notes:
## Arrival
Land at **PEK**, clear customs, and take the **Airport Express** to Dongzhimen (¥25, 20 min). Check into the hutong inn and drop luggage.

## Evening
Walk through **Nanluoguxiang** — grab jianbing (savoury crepes) from a street cart and explore the lantern-lit alley. Early night; jet lag is real.
tagline: Touch down and wander the hutongs

notes:
## Morning
Full day at the **Forbidden City** and Tiananmen. Be at the South Gate by **8 am** to beat the crowds — book tickets in advance online (¥60 pp). Walk the full north–south axis; don't miss the **Clock Exhibition Hall**.

## Afternoon
Stroll through **Jingshan Park** for the classic rooftop view back over the golden rooftops.

## Dinner
**Dadong Roast Duck** — order the crispy suckling duck, not just the Peking duck, if they have it.
tagline: Forbidden City, Jingshan Park and duck

notes:
## Great Wall
- Take a private taxi to **Mutianyu** (less crowded than Badaling, more dramatic than Simatai for a day trip).
- Cable car up, toboggan run down — genuinely fun.
- The wall dates to the Ming dynasty; walk east from the cable car for views and almost no people.
- Pack lunch or eat at the village below.

## Afternoon
Back in the hutongs — rent bikes and cycle the **Second Ring Road** canal path.
tagline: Great Wall views and a toboggan run

notes:
## Morning
**Temple of Heaven** — arrive early for the extraordinary sight of elderly Beijingers practising tai chi, ballroom dancing and opera in the park.

## Afternoon
Keep this intentionally light: late lunch back in the hutongs, last-minute errands, showers and repacking before checkout.

## Evening
Head to **Beijing West** with plenty of buffer, eat near the station, and board the sleeper to Xi'an. Book **soft sleeper (软卧)** well in advance (12306 or Trip.com); for a group of seven, reserve as early as tickets open.
tagline: Temple of Heaven and the Xi'an sleeper

notes:
## Arrival
Arrive Xi'an in the morning by overnight sleeper. Drop bags, check in if the room is ready, and keep the day deliberately flexible after the train night.

## Afternoon
Wander the **Muslim Quarter** at an easy pace and eat your way from the **Drum Tower** toward the **Great Mosque**:
- **Roujiamo**
- **Biangbiang noodles**
- Fresh pomegranate juice

## Evening
Take a gentle sunset walk on the **city walls** if energy is good; otherwise just have an early night.
tagline: Muslim Quarter feast and city-wall sunset

notes:
## Terracotta Army
- Leave by **8 am** by prebooked car or tourist bus from the station.
- **Pit 1** is overwhelming in scale; **Pit 3** shows the command structure.
- Don't miss the museum building housing the **bronze chariots**.
- A guide is worth it here.
- Return to the city by late afternoon.

## Evening
Keep the evening simple: tea, showers and a **Defachang** dumpling banquet rather than another major sight.
tagline: Terracotta Warriors and dumpling night

notes:
## Morning
**Shaanxi History Museum** — one of China's finest. Reserve the free timed slot in advance and go first thing with passports; queues build quickly. Head straight for the Tang gold and silver collection.

## Afternoon
**Big Wild Goose Pagoda** and the surrounding square. If everyone still has energy, browse **Shuyuanmen** calligraphy street on the way back.

## Evening
Free evening for rooftop drinks, laundry or a second run at the Muslim Quarter.
tagline: Tang treasures and pagoda views

notes:
## Travel
Morning **G/D train** from **Xi'an North** to **Chengdu East**, then Chengdu East to **Emeishan** station (~5 hrs total) — book in advance (12306 or Trip.com).

## Afternoon
Check into the lodge near **Baoguo Temple** at the mountain's base. Explore the temple complex and buy any last snacks, rain gear or warm layers here rather than higher up the mountain.

## Evening
Early vegetarian dinner and an early night. Leave big bags at the lodge tomorrow; only take an overnight bag up the mountain.
tagline: Train to Emei and temple calm
travel: Train Xi'an North → Chengdu East → Emeishan, ~5 hrs

notes:
## Lower mountain day
Take the **eco-bus** up from Baoguo to **Wuxiangang** so you skip the road slog, then hike the prettiest lower section: **Qingyin Pavilion**, **Yixiantian (One Line of Sky)** gorge and the forest trails around the streams.

## Afternoon
Descend back to the base rather than forcing the full 99 Bends. Use the extra time for a hot spring soak, early dinner and a proper sleep before the summit day.

## Night
Sleep again at the Baoguo lodge so the group starts the high-altitude push rested.
tagline: Forest trails and monastery peace

notes:
## Summit day
Leave early by **eco-bus** to **Leidongping**, then walk the final mountain section to the cable car. Take the cable car up to the **Golden Summit** unless conditions are exceptional and everyone still wants the stairs.

## Afternoon
Visit **Huazang Temple** and the gold-plated **Puxian Bodhisattva** statue, then keep the rest of the day unhurried at altitude.

## Sunset
The sea of clouds is the payoff. Bring warm layers; it can drop close to freezing in October.

## Night
Sleep at the summit guesthouse so sunrise tomorrow is effortless.
tagline: Golden Summit and sunset above the clouds

notes:
## Sunrise
Wake before dawn for sunrise at the **Golden Summit** — and if conditions line up, watch for the famous Buddha's Halo in the clouds below.

## Descent
Take the cable car down and the **eco-bus** back to the base. Collect the stored luggage, shower if the lodge offers day-use rooms, and have an early lunch before leaving.

## Leshan stop
On the way north, stop in **Leshan** for the easier panoramic **boat view** of the Giant Buddha rather than trying to cram in the full cliff walk after the mountain.

## Evening
Continue by high-speed train from **Leshan** to **Chongqing North**. Book the train in advance and expect a late but manageable arrival.
tagline: Sunrise on Emei and the Leshan Buddha
travel: Leshan → Chongqing North by high-speed rail, ~1.5 hrs

notes:
## Morning
Slow Chongqing morning after the late arrival. Sleep in, find noodles near the hotel, and keep the first half of the day unstructured.

## Afternoon
Explore the central peninsula on foot: **Jiefangbei**, the stepped lanes around **Shibati**, and the riverfront viewpoints.

## Dusk
Head to **Hongya Cave** for the lights coming on over the **Jialing River**.

## Dinner
Chongqing **hot-pot** — order the **yuan-yang** half-and-half pot if anyone is heat-sensitive.
tagline: Chongqing neon and hot-pot

notes:
## Travel
Flight from **Chongqing Jiangbei (CKG)** to **Zhangjiajie Hehua (DYG)**, ~2 hrs — book in advance.

## Afternoon
Transfer to **Wulingyuan**, check in, and keep this as a genuine arrival day. Walk the creekside streets, sort tomorrow's park tickets, and turn in early.

## Evening
Dinner near the hotel rather than attempting a rushed park detour after the flight.
tagline: Arrive in Zhangjiajie and settle in

notes:
## Morning
Full day in **Zhangjiajie National Forest Park**. Start at **sunrise** on the **Yuanjiajie** plateau (most dramatic lighting on the columns). Walk the cliff-hanging **Tianzi Mountain** trails. Take the **Bailong Elevator** (the world's tallest outdoor elevator, **330 m**, glass-sided) down through the cliff face — genuinely terrifying.

## Afternoon
**Jinbian Stream** canyon trail — a **7 km** riverside walk through the densest concentration of pillars. Pack snacks; food stalls are limited inside the park.
tagline: Avatar peaks, cliffs and glass elevator

notes:
## Full day at Tianmen Mountain
Prebook a timed entry and give **Tianmen Mountain** the whole day; trying to pair it with the Grand Canyon is what makes this area feel frantic.

Ride the long cable car up, visit the cliff walkways, descend via the escalators to **Tianmen Cave**, and only tackle the **999 stairs** if the group still feels fresh.

## Evening
Easy dinner and an early night before the Guilin transfer tomorrow.
tagline: Tianmen Mountain and Heaven's Gate

notes:
## Travel
Flight from **Zhangjiajie Hehua (DYG)** to **Guilin Liangjiang (KWL)**, ~1.5–2 hrs. From the airport, transfer straight to **Yangshuo** and check into the guesthouse.

## Afternoon
Keep it easy after the travel: stretch your legs along the **Li River** and browse **West Street** rather than trying to squeeze in countryside cycling immediately.

## Evening
**Impression Liu Sanjie** is a good first-night splurge if everyone is up for it; otherwise save the energy for tomorrow.
tagline: Arrive in Yangshuo by the river

notes:
## Yangshuo countryside day
Rent bicycles and ride the **Yulong River** route through rice paddies and karst hills.

## Afternoon
Do the peaceful **bamboo raft** section on the Yulong after lunch, then leave the rest of the day open for cafes, massages or a nap by the river.

## Dinner
Try **Yangshuo beer fish** tonight.
tagline: Yangshuo cycling and bamboo rafting

notes:
## Li River cruise
Leave **Yangshuo** early by prebooked transfer to the **Guilin** pier, then take the classic downstream **Li River cruise** back to Yangshuo. It is an early start, but it fixes the route logic and lets you keep the most iconic river scenery.

## Afternoon
Disembark back in Yangshuo and keep the rest of the day gentle: coffee, shopping or one last wander through the villages.

## Evening
Farewell riverside dinner and pack for Hong Kong.
tagline: Li River cruise and one last Yangshuo night
travel: Early transfer Yangshuo → Guilin pier; cruise returns to Yangshuo

notes:
## Travel
From Yangshuo, allow about **1 hr** back to **Guilin Liangjiang Airport**. For a group of seven, a **direct flight to Hong Kong** is the least stressful option; only use the Shenzhen rail-border combo if flight times are bad.

## Afternoon
Check in, then take the **Star Ferry** across the harbour to Kowloon and walk the **Tsim Sha Tsui** waterfront at dusk.

## Dinner
**Temple Street Night Market** for egg waffles, curry fishballs and HK-style milk tea.
tagline: Harbour lights and Hong Kong nights
travel: Direct flight Guilin → Hong Kong preferred

notes:
## Morning
**Victoria Peak** — ride the century-old **Peak Tram** to the summit (go early before cloud rolls in). The 360-degree view of the harbour, islands and mountains is the defining Hong Kong panorama. Walk back down via the tree-lined **Lugard Road** circuit.

## Afternoon
Explore **Central** and **Sheung Wan** on foot — **Hollywood Road** antique shops, the **Man Mo Temple**, and **PMQ** creative market. Dim sum lunch at **Lin Heung Tea House** (old-school trolley service, arrive before **11 am**).
tagline: Victoria Peak and dim sum in Central

notes:
## Morning
Keep the final day simple: breakfast in **Sheung Wan**, a last browse around **Graham Street** or **Hollywood Road**, and back to the hotel to collect bags.

## Departure
For an evening flight from **Shenzhen**, prebook a **cross-border car** or leave by early afternoon via **MTR** to **Futian** or **Lo Wu** with plenty of buffer. Do not plan Lantau or another major excursion today.
tagline: Hong Kong farewell and flight home
